subject: Public Health
title: Toxoid Vaccines
description: A micro-lesson that makes Toxoid Vaccines usable.

hook.text: Some diseases don't hurt you with the bacteria—they hurt you with the poison the bacteria makes.

buildup.text: Tetanus and diphtheria bacteria release toxins that cause muscle spasms and throat swelling respectively.

discovery.text: Toxoid vaccines use inactivated toxins—teaching the immune system to neutralize the poison, not the bacterium.

twist.text: Your body learns to destroy the toxin before it causes damage—even if the bacteria are still present.

climax.text: The DTaP vaccine combines diphtheria and tetanus toxoids with pertussis antigens—three protections in one shot.

punchline.text: These vaccines don't fight the bacteria. They disarm its weapon.
